title: Insulin Discovered
description: Frederick Banting and Charles Best discover insulin at the University of Toronto, transforming T1D from a death sentence into a manageable condition.

title: First Insulin Injection in a Human
description: 14-year-old Leonard Thompson becomes the first person to receive insulin injection, marking the beginning of modern diabetes treatment.

title: Synthetic Human Insulin Created
description: Genentech produces the first recombinant human insulin using E. coli bacteria, ending dependence on animal-derived insulin.

title: First Insulin Pump
description: The first portable insulin pump is developed, offering continuous subcutaneous insulin delivery as an alternative to multiple daily injections.

title: T1D Identified as Autoimmune Disease
description: Researchers discover that T1D is caused by the immune system attacking insulin-producing beta cells, fundamentally changing the approach to finding a cure.

title: Edmonton Protocol — Islet Transplant Breakthrough
description: Dr. James Shapiro and team at University of Alberta achieve insulin independence in 7 T1D patients using a novel islet transplant protocol, proving that cell replacement can work.

title: First Continuous Glucose Monitor (CGM)
description: The first CGM system is FDA-approved, allowing continuous tracking of glucose levels instead of relying solely on finger pricks.

title: JDRF Launches Artificial Pancreas Project
description: JDRF (now Breakthrough T1D) launches a major initiative to develop closed-loop automated insulin delivery systems.

title: DIY Closed-Loop Movement Begins
description: The #WeAreNotWaiting movement creates open-source artificial pancreas systems (OpenAPS), proving that closed-loop technology works and pushing industry forward.

title: First FDA-Approved Closed-Loop System
description: Medtronic's MiniMed 670G becomes the first FDA-approved hybrid closed-loop insulin delivery system.

title: Harvard Creates Functional Beta Cells from Stem Cells
description: Dr. Douglas Melton at Harvard generates the first large quantities of functional human beta cells from stem cells in the lab — the foundational work behind Vertex's zimislecel.

title: Vertex Acquires Semma Therapeutics for $950M
description: Vertex Pharmaceuticals acquires Semma Therapeutics (founded on Dr. Melton's work) for $950 million, signaling major pharma commitment to stem cell cure.

title: Nobel Prize for CRISPR
description: Jennifer Doudna and Emmanuelle Charpentier win the Nobel Prize for CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing — the technology that could make immune-invisible beta cells possible.

title: First Patient Receives VX-880 Stem Cells
description: The first patient in the VX-880 clinical trial receives stem cell-derived islet cells, beginning the pivotal journey toward a cell replacement cure.

title: Teplizumab (Tzield) FDA Approved
description: The FDA approves teplizumab — the first-ever disease-modifying therapy for T1D. It can delay the onset of clinical T1D by more than 2 years in at-risk individuals.

title: Lantidra FDA Approved — First Cell Therapy for T1D
description: The FDA approves Lantidra (donislecel), the first-ever allogeneic islet cell therapy for T1D, for adults with severe hypoglycemia unawareness.

title: Omnipod 5 Cleared for Ages 2+
description: The tubeless Omnipod 5 automated insulin delivery system receives FDA clearance for children as young as 2 years old.

title: iLet Bionic Pancreas FDA Cleared
description: Beta Bionics' iLet Bionic Pancreas receives FDA clearance — the first fully automated insulin pump that requires no carb counting.

title: First CRISPR-Edited Islet Cells Transplanted Without Immunosuppression
description: Sana Biotechnology's UP421 — CRISPR gene-edited hypoimmune islet cells — are transplanted into a human for the first time WITHOUT immunosuppression. Cells survive and produce insulin.

title: VX-264 Encapsulated Cells Discontinued
description: Vertex discontinues the VX-264 encapsulation device program after it fails to produce sufficient insulin. A setback for the encapsulation approach.

title: Sana's Gene-Edited Cells Still Working at 6 Months
description: 6-month results confirm that Sana's CRISPR-edited islet cells continue to survive and produce insulin without immunosuppression. Results published in the New England Journal of Medicine.

title: Zimislecel Enters Phase 3 — 83% Insulin Independence
description: Vertex reports that 83% of full-dose participants in the VX-880 trial are insulin-independent at one year. Phase 3 trial with 50 participants is underway. FDA submission planned for 2026.

title: Teplizumab Accepted for Stage 3 T1D Review
description: FDA accepts Sanofi's application for teplizumab in newly diagnosed Stage 3 T1D for expedited review, potentially expanding access beyond prevention to treatment.

title: Stanford Cures T1D in Mice with Hybrid Immune Approach
description: Stanford researchers cure T1D in mice using combined blood stem cell + islet transplant that creates a hybrid immune system, eliminating the need for immunosuppression.

title: Dexcom G7 15-Day Sensor Available
description: Dexcom launches the G7 15-day sensor in the US — the longest-lasting FDA-approved wearable CGM, reducing sensor changes from every 10 days to every 15 days.
